212.reason: [This incorrectly assumes the char const is only one character long and already includes closing quote; it should start with just "'", then read the content]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This incorrectly assumes the char const is only one character long and already includes closing quote; it should start with just "'", then read the content]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This incorrectly initializes wordname with only the opening quote and the current character (which is the quote itself), leading to malformed char constant representation. It should start by consuming the opening quote and then read the actual character content.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [This incorrectly assumes the current character is the entire char literal; it should start with just the opening quote and then read the content]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [This line incorrectly initializes the character constant string. At this point, curChar is the opening single quote ('), so wordname becomes "''" (two quotes) immediately, which is wrong. The correct approach is to start with just the opening quote and then append the actual character content and closing quote during parsing. This leads to malformed token representation for CharConst.]
